title: Floral Caramelized Croatian Oats Porridge
description: A dairy-free and vegan version of a traditional dish that features floral notes with a touch of caramelization for a lazy Sunday meal.

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: Optional for creaminess, ensure it's unsweetened to avoid excess sweetness.

ingredients.name: Water (or Vegan Milk)
ingredients.notes: Use as a substitute for coconut milk if unavailable, ensuring it's unsweetened.

ingredients.name: Melted Coconut Oil or Olive Oil
Vanilla Bean Powder
ingredients.notes: For added floral and caramelization effects, use vanilla extract as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Coconut Sugar or Other Sweetener of Choice (Optional)

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the oat groats and water. Mix gently with a spoon; do not stir continuously as you want to keep some texture in the oats.
Once mixed, pour in coconut milk (or unsweetened plant-based milk), and add a pinch of salt for flavor enhancement.
Add the melted oil and vanilla bean powder. Gently mix them into the saucepan’s contents without stirring vigorously or they will clump together and become less appealing visually.
Transfer the mixture to an oven-safe dish with a glass lid, then place it in the preheated oven for about 45 minutes or until the center of the oat groats is fully cooked but slightly firm.
Remove from heat once done. Let it cool down before serving.

For added caramelization effects (optional), you can spoon out some of this cooked mixture into a shallow dish, drizzle with coconut sugar, and bake in the oven at 350°F for about 10 minutes until caramelized to your liking.
Serve the porridge warm. If desired, garnish it with fresh berries or a sprinkle of unsweetened shredded coconut.
